title: Human 'mini-brains' reveal protein GRAMD1B's potential role in neurodegeneration
summary: Researchers at The Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center and College of Medicine have discovered a new way that neurons act in neurodegeneration by using human neural organoids—also known as "mini-brain" models—from patients with frontotemporal lobar degeneration (FTLD).

title: Nanoparticles Cross Blood-Brain Barrier in Mouse Study
summary: Engineered nanoparticles deliver anti-inflammatory drugs across the blood-brain barrier, opening doors to treating neurodegenerative diseases.

title: Water Filter With Nanoscale Channels Removes Stubborn PFAS
summary: Researchers have developed a filter that effectively removes small PFAS molecules from water, overcoming a major hurdle in current water filtration technology.

title: Asian Immune Diversity Atlas Enhances Precision Medicine
summary: Researchers create the first Asian Immune Diversity Atlas, analyzing over 1.2 million immune cells from diverse Asian populations to advance precision medicine.

title: DA: Vaccine center to help curb transboundary animal diseases
summary: MANILA – The Department of Agriculture (DA) on Wednesday announced the launch of the state-of-the-art vaccine unit at the Center for Transboundary Animal Diseases (CenTrAD) in Nueva Ecija province. The DA-funded CenTrAD is a joint project between the Bureau of Animal Industry and Central Luzon…

title: What happens if you significantly increase dietary folic acid intake?
summary: Increased dietary folic acid intake may lead to adverse health effects, including inflammation and neurotoxicity, highlighting the need for optimal levels.

title: QRICH1 Protein: New Target for Immune System Treatments
summary: Researchers discover QRICH1 protein's role in regulating T cells, offering new opportunities for targeted immunotherapies in cancer and autoimmune diseases.

title: Loss of Protective Fats Raises Cardiovascular Disease Risk
summary: New research from Weill Cornell Medicine reveals that reduced levels of ceramides, protective fats in blood vessels, can increase cardiovascular disease risk.

title: Galway students lead groundbreaking dementia and diabetes study
summary: Two Galway-based medical students are at the forefront of a major study into how drugs targeting diabetes can lower dementia risk.
